progress.modelNote: every bar reserves its last 20% as a test & rework buffer (hatched) — it only fills once the stream is tested in production; build progress alone can never reach 100%
progress.scopeNote: scope expanded 2026-06-11 after the first full review: admin ops wiring, portal redesigns, localization, DB-integration tests, an integrations workstream (SMS/OTP, banking, push) and a release & store workstream (iOS/Android bundling) added — overall % dropped accordingly. New productroadmap.md items added after this date lower the affected workstream automatically.

release.note: Path from a working app to live in the stores. Externally gated — needs Apple Developer and Google Play accounts and passes external review.

dist cert + provisioning profile + ASC API key all in place
build #4 — OTA-capable (expo-updates, 'production' channel); signed with your own Apple cert

steps.label: TestFlight
steps.note: build #4 uploaded to App Store Connect — Apple processing; OTA now live for JS changes

testAccess.warning: Pre-launch demo access — production holds test/seed data only. These credentials will be removed and the DEV_OTP bypass disabled before go-live (tracked on the roadmap). Don't enter real personal or payment data.
testAccess.note: Everything needed for an end-to-end test: log in, run a purchase, repay, reconcile, settle.

surfaces.name: customer app
surfaces.runNote: open in Expo Go (no hosted URL yet — store builds on roadmap)

decisions.title: One monthly bill
decisions.detail: all purchases roll into a single payment reference due at month-end — one transfer clears everything

decisions.title: Month-end due dates
decisions.detail: every installment lands on a calendar month-end, never odd mid-month dates

decisions.title: Plan belongs to the merchant
decisions.detail: stores choose 1 or 3 monthly payments — shoppers never pick a plan at checkout

decisions.title: Customers pay Qisto, never the store
decisions.detail: repayments go to Qisto by bank transfer and are reconciled by reference — merchants are settled separately

decisions.title: Zero interest, merchant-financed
decisions.detail: customers pay no interest or fees — Qisto earns a 7% merchant fee per sale

decisions.title: Credit ladder
decisions.detail: down payment shrinks as customers repay on time — 60% for new customers down to 25% for trusted ones

shipped.text: iOS customer app live in TestFlight (build #4) + EAS Update (OTA) enabled — design & logic changes now ship to installed apps without an App Store release
admin console & merchant portal rebranded to the Qisto design system and switched to auto-deploy — every push to main ships both, at the same URLs
full repayment cycle live — customer reports a transfer, admin reconciles, ledger posts, credit restores, loan closes
consolidated monthly bill — one reference per customer per month across all purchases and merchants
mobile app wired to the live API — login, KYC, wallet, scan-to-pay, monthly statement — with the Ink Royale design and animated splash
month-end installment model — plans owned by merchants, schedules generated automatically
critical role-check vulnerability found by adversarial audit, fixed and verified in production the same day
double-entry accounting ledger with balanced-posting guarantees behind every money movement

now.label: gate dev login bypass to non-production
wire admin console to live endpoints
pick one settlement model, align docs with code

next.label: DB-backed integration tests — recon, settlement, idempotency
Arabic + RTL + SYP amounts across the customer app

qaReports.note: Deep audits live in the qisto repo qa-reports/ folder; open cases are GitHub issues.

reports.title: Money-math end-to-end audit
reports.verdict: Core math verified correct: live production scenarios across 3 merchant configs, 41-invariant checklist, 400k-case property tests, full ledger balance. 4 HIGH + 4 MED hardening cases opened (failure/race paths, not wrong math).

openCases.title: Settlement approve/pay race — possible double merchant payout
Delivery accrual fire-and-forget — lost ledger postings on crash
Zero-amount guards: $0 loans never close; 0%-fee settlements wedge
Idempotency scoping+TTL · bank-amount matching · term locking · reconcile batch isolation
